dimensions.rationale: All twenty required outcomes are explicit, project-neutral, non-duplicated, and mapped exactly once into the current plan; the routing and shared adapter baseline additions close the prior behavioral gaps.

Included, excluded, and deferred scope remains explicit and consistent with a standalone lifecycle kit, four initial surface adapters, synthetic-only repository content, and no remote publication authority.
Every requirement has one unique acceptance id and one unique evidence id; the corrected neutrality, routing, and adapter baseline promises now have reciprocal criteria and final gates.

External trust ownership, temporary conformance keys, deterministic capability fallback, reviewer independence, unsupported operations, adapter maturity, resource limits, and zero-origin-information controls are decision-complete and fail closed.

The specification and plan define deterministic signature negatives, stage and role routing cases, synthetic adapter inclusion, live verification promotion, exact usage attribution, release evidence, and independent final reconciliation.

Requirement wording, developer overview, architecture contracts, workstreams, traceability matrix, acceptance checklist, and release rules agree on signing authority, routing semantics, and EXPERIMENTAL versus VERIFIED behavior.

findings.message: REQ-NEUTRALITY now defines detached Ed25519 authority and attestation verification, host-owned external trust roots, signer and subject binding, freshness, zero-result conditions, temporary conformance keys, and fail-closed behavior; AC-NEUTRALITY and final gates test the same contract.
findings.requiredAction: No further specification change is required; preserve the external trust-root, temporary-key, freshness, subject-binding, and reciprocal evidence semantics during plan freeze and implementation.

findings.message: REQ-ROUTING now enumerates every semantic stage and role, keeps profiles in external provider-neutral configuration, allows one or different host models, preserves mandatory capabilities and independence during deterministic fallback, fails closed when unavailable, and attributes the exact selection in usage receipts.
findings.requiredAction: No further specification change is required; retain REQ-ROUTING, AC-ROUTING, EV-ROUTING, and their exact task projection as one reciprocal contract.

findings.message: REQ-ADAPTER-BASELINE now fixes the minimum install, discovery, envelope, capability, fail-closed, and synthetic conformance contract; the four adapter requirements inherit it, EXPERIMENTAL inclusion is explicitly release-acceptable, and VERIFIED requires fresh live proof of the complete operation matrix.
findings.requiredAction: No further specification change is required; keep the shared baseline authoritative and prevent any surface-specific requirement or maturity claim from weakening it.

summary: READY_TO_PLAN after a full second-round review. Structural validation binds hash 20179dbbf8c0c16cf3a3e82b3ceb19e9a397f033002ad7a2d0609fa2df1ec567; all twenty required requirements have unique acceptance and evidence links, SPEC-001 through SPEC-003 are resolved in both specification and plan projections, and all six mandatory review dimensions pass with no open blocking, high, or medium finding.
